title: Search & Replace Regex
author: hon4
body:
**App Version:**
2025.1.3

**Affected Device(s):**
Xiaomi Redmi Note 8 (GINKGO), Android 9 / MIUI 11.0.12.0

**Describe the bug**
When replacing with Regex Enabled the Regex works only in find field and not in the replace field. For example replacing "\n" with "\r\n" the find (`\n`) works as newline but in replace (`\r\n`) I get as text not Windows newline.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Replace with Regex enabled using Regex in the replace field.

**Expected behavior**
It should recognize the regex in both find and replace Fields but it recognize it only in the find field.

title: Tab button in extended keyboard.
author: hon4
body:
**App Version:**
2025.1.3

**Affected Device(s):**
Xiaomi Redmi Note 8 (GINKGO), Android 9 / MIUI 11.0.12.0

**Describe the bug**
When clicking the `Tab` button in the extended keyboard I get four (4) spaces instead of one Tab.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Click Tab, you get 4 spaces.

**Expected behavior**
It should give one tab instead of four spaces.

title: Data loss without warning if file changed by another process
author: Markus00000
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

1. Edit a file in Squircle CE and leave the tab open
2. File is changed by another process (e.g. a file synchronization app syncs changes made on another device)
3. Without manually reloading the tab, saving the file in Squircle CE will overwrite the previously synced changes

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Some editors check if a file has been changed when entering a tab or before saving. This behavior is implemented in Notepad++, Vim/Neovim, [Editor](https://github.com/billthefarmer/editor) and others. When returning to a tab, the user is asked whether to reload the file. When saving a tab, the user is asked whether to overwrite changes.

I can attest that having users manually reload is error-prone…

Not sure about the best way to implement this. Checking last modified timestamps would be efficient, or, if that is reliable:

* [FileObserver](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/FileObserver)
* [ContentObserver](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/database/ContentObserver)

title: Add Android Manifest to Allow Setting as Default Editor for MIME types ie text/plain text/x-csharp
author: solutioneeer
body: Current Android versions of Squircle-CE do not allow themselves to be assigned as the default file handler for most source code types.  Apparently this is because an Android app has to explicitly state in a manifest component which MIME types it can handle, and the Android OS then uses that info to proffer which apps are 'suitable' to be a default opener for file-type 'X'.  MIME is important as Android does not use file extensions for app assignment.  If I read it right, Intents are key.  https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/manifest-intro

title: Add HTML Preview Feature
author: channel101
body:
Hello 👋  

First of all, thank you for maintaining Squircle-CE. It’s a great editor and I really enjoy using it.

I’d like to request a new feature: **HTML Preview**.

## Feature Description
It would be very helpful to have a built-in HTML preview that allows users to quickly see how their HTML renders, without leaving the editor.

## Use Cases
- Quickly preview static HTML files while editing  
- Frontend development and prototyping  
- Educational use (learning HTML/CSS)  
- Faster iteration without switching to an external browser  

## Suggested Behavior (Optional)
- A toggleable preview panel (side-by-side or bottom view)  
- Auto-refresh on file save or on content change  
- Basic support for linked CSS and local assets  
- Option to open preview in an external browser  

I believe this feature would significantly improve productivity for users working with web content and make Squircle-CE even more powerful.

Thank you for considering this request 🙏  
If needed, I’d be happy to provide more details or test the feature.

title: Add Workspace from Internal storage
author: computerconsultingpeople
body:
*Please consider making a Pull Request if you are capable of doing so.*

**App Version:**
2025.1.3(10028)

**Affected Device(s):**
Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 3  with Android 15.0

**Describe the bug**
Attempt to add an SMB folder using  CIFS Document Provider results in No Such File or directory

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Install https://github.com/wa2c/cifs-documents-provider and configure

In editor
Add->Internal Storage->CIFS Documents Provider
Select the folder and then pressing the button "USE THIS FOLDER"
A Dialogue "Allow Squircle CE to access files in [Folder name selected] -> CANCEL | ALLOW
Pressing ALLOW generates error No Such File or Directory content://com.wa2c.android.cifsdocumentprovider.documents/tree/ ..........


**Expected behavior**
The folder should be added to the list

title: Missing character with word wrap and line numbers
author: Markus00000
body: **App Version:** 2025.1.3 (10028) **Affected Device(s):** Xiaomi Redmi Note 12 4G with Android 15 **Describe the bug** With word wrap and line numbers enabled, the last character before a line break can be missing. In this example, the word *here* is missing its second *e* when wrapping: **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Enable word wrap 2. Enable line numbers 3. Enter text that wraps 4. Add/remove characters until a character disappears **Expected behavior** Wrap the whole word. Always show all characters.

title: Physical keyboard input fails for Traditional Chinese in Squircle-CE (on-screen Gboard works fine)
author: will-fred-japan
body:
**App Version:**
2025.1.3

**Affected Device(s):**
Device: Lenovo IdeaTab Plus (Android 15)

**Describe the bug**
- Physical keyboard input works in Chrome and other apps like Google Keep.
- In Squircle-CE, physical keyboard input fails under certain conditions.
- Output is English letters only, not Chinese characters.
- Gboard (Google Keyboard) works without problem.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
- Connect physical keyboard (USB/Bluetooth) to Android tablet (Lenovo IdeaTab Plus).
- Start Squircle-CE while keyboard is already connected.
- Set Gboard as default input method, Traditional Chinese enabled (Zhuyin/Pinyin/Cangjie).
- Try typing Traditional Chinese in Squircle-CE.

**Expected behavior**
Physical keyboard should input Chinese characters correctly, same as Chrome/other apps.
